description: 10-turn conversation where a user describes a project that evolves. Tests whether the agent stores the right things, updates when facts change, and retrieves when needed.

initial_memories.content: User is a frontend developer named Jordan. Works at a startup called Nexus.

turns.user_message: Hey! I'm starting a new project — building a real-time dashboard for our IoT sensors. We have about 500 sensors sending data every 10 seconds.
turns.response_context: User is starting a new IoT dashboard project with 500 sensors at 10-second intervals.

turns.user_message: I'm thinking about using WebSockets for the real-time updates. What do you think?
turns.response_context: User asking for architectural advice. Should retrieve project context to give informed answer.

turns.user_message: Good points. We'll go with WebSockets using Socket.io. The backend will be Node.js with Express.
turns.response_context: User made a technology decision. This should be stored.

turns.user_message: For the database, I was thinking InfluxDB since we're dealing with time-series data. Does that make sense?
turns.response_context: User considering InfluxDB. Should retrieve project context to give informed advice.

turns.user_message: Actually, after thinking about it more, let's use TimescaleDB instead. We already have PostgreSQL experience on the team and TimescaleDB is a PostgreSQL extension, so it'll be easier.
turns.response_context: User changed their mind from InfluxDB to TimescaleDB. The decision and reasoning should be stored.

turns.user_message: Oh, I forgot to mention — the number of sensors is actually going to be around 2000 by next quarter. We just signed a big contract.
turns.response_context: User correcting earlier information. The sensor count memory should be updated, not duplicated.

turns.user_message: Can you remind me what tech stack we decided on so far?
turns.response_context: User asking for recall. Should retrieve and summarize: WebSockets/Socket.io, Node.js/Express, TimescaleDB, 2000 sensors.

turns.user_message: Perfect. For the frontend, I'll use React with D3.js for the visualizations. The charts need to update in real-time as new data comes in.
Thanks for all the help today. Quick question — what's our sensor count again? I need it for the capacity planning doc.
turns.response_context: Tests whether the agent stored the UPDATE from turn 6. Must say 2000, not 500.

turns.user_message: Great. Let's wrap up. One more thing — we're targeting a 3-month timeline for the MVP. Launch date is end of June.
turns.response_context: New project timeline information worth storing.

final_memory_should_contain: 3-month timeline, MVP by end of June
final_memory_should_not_contain: 500 sensors as current count (should be updated to 2000)
InfluxDB as the chosen database (was changed to TimescaleDB)
correct_epistemic_behavior: Store new factual information (project details, tech decisions, timeline)
Update rather than duplicate when facts change (sensor count 500→2000)
Retrieve when the user asks for recall or needs context-informed advice
Do NOT store every conversational message — skip acknowledgments and questions
At turn 9, correctly recall 2000 sensors (not the original 500)
Handle the InfluxDB→TimescaleDB change cleanly (store TimescaleDB, not InfluxDB)
incorrect_behaviors: Storing every turn as a new memory (over-storing)
Keeping 500 sensors after the user said 2000
Storing InfluxDB as the database choice (it was changed to TimescaleDB)
Failing to retrieve context when the user asks 'remind me what we decided'
Not recognizing turn 6 as an update to existing information

key_claims.claim: The system correctly stores new project information
The system updates sensor count from 500 to 2000
The system stores InfluxDB as the database
The system retrieves context when user asks for recall
